meaning: vt.(使)快速转动,捻弄vi.转动,旋转n.转动,万能钥匙,旋转的东西
examples: Don't slouch or twirl your hair once you're there.
When I twirl this around, it produces a particular tone.
A small boy appears utterly confused as the ears twirl apparently at random above him.
But it would also cause Teddy Roosevelt to twirl in his grave.
Not every kid in the world gets a chance to swim, twirl around the uneven bars, dig a volleyball out of sand or swing a bat.
